Broadcast company expands connected TV service across India

A broadcast company expanded its connected TV service across India after completing a regional pilot. The platform combines linear channels, FAST programming, and on demand discovery in one television guide. Executives said the rollout supports modern advertising formats and gives viewers a consistent interface across televisions and set top boxes. The service will initially focus on major cities and add more local content partners during the coming months. The company also described new measurement tools for broadcasters, agencies, and advertisers. Analysts expect connected TV usage to continue growing as broadband availability improves and more households adopt internet enabled television devices.
